pattern: Processing nodes in increasing value and union-merging only through already-activated neighbours lets each component, when it reaches a value, count the pairs of equal maxima it can join.
hints: A good path's endpoints share a value that is the maximum along the path, so think about adding nodes from smallest value upward.
When you activate all nodes of a given value, two of them can form a good path only if they are already connected through nodes of value at most that.
Sort nodes by value, union a node with already-seen neighbours, and for each value count, within every component, how many activated nodes share that value, adding pairs plus the singletons.

edge_inputs.description: one long good path plus singletons
equal endpoints but a larger value blocks the path
all same value path counts every pair

stress.description: random tree via a parent assignment with small value range to create many equal-value pairs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    vals = [rng.randint(0, 8) for _ in range(n)]
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        p = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([p, i])
    return [vals, edges]
stress.note: parent-based construction guarantees a valid tree; small values force many good paths
